perturbation.original_text: Operator shall perform Owner's Tax and financial accounting with respect to the Gathering System, except to the extent that Operator is prohibited or prevented from doing so by Law, or by the administrative practice of any taxing office, or by the fact that to do so requires information that is in the possession of Owner, but not of Operator and that Owner cannot legally furnish to Operator (all of which are herein called "Preventing Factors").
perturbation.changed_text: Service Provider shall perform Owner's Tax and financial accounting with respect to the Gathering System, except to the extent that Service Provider is prohibited or prevented from doing so by Law, or by the administrative practice of any taxing office, or by the fact that to do so requires information that is in the possession of Owner, but not of Service Provider and that Owner cannot legally furnish to Service Provider (all of which are herein called "Impeding Factors").
perturbation.explanation: In Section 6.3.1, the term 'Operator' is replaced with 'Service Provider', and 'Preventing Factors' is replaced with 'Impeding Factors.' This change causes confusion because the contract consistently refers to the managing party as 'Operator' in all other sections, including the definitions in Article I and the engagement clause in Article II. This introduces uncertainty as to whether the accounting duties apply to the 'Operator' as defined or to a potentially different entity referred to as 'Service Provider'. In addition, changing 'Preventing Factors' to 'Impeding Factors' creates a contradiction by introducing a new undefined term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the request of Operator, Owner shall (except to the extent it is prohibited by Law from doing so) promptly (i) furnish Operator with any information needed by Operator in order to carry out its obligations under Section 6.3, (ii) cooperate with Operator in minimizing Taxes and (iii) execute such agency, power-of-attorney or other similar documents reasonably required by Operator in order to carry out its duties under this Section 6.3. If, at any time, Operator determines that because of one or more Preventing Factors it cannot perform its obligations under Section 6.3, then Operator shall notify Owner in writing, specifying the Preventing Factors concerned and the Taxes and the Governmental Authorities to which such Preventing Factors apply. Any incremental Tax or penalty resulting from Owner's failure to comply with this Section 6.3.3 shall be the responsibility of Owner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Operator' with 'Service Provider' and 'Preventing Factors' with 'Impeding Factors'. The contradicted text consistently refers to 'Operator' and 'Preventing Factors'. Since the contract refers to the managing party as 'Operator', changing it to 'Service Provider' creates ambiguity and uncertainty as to which entity has the accounting duties. Also the contradicted text uses the term 'Preventing Factors', but the Changed text defines 'Impeding Factors'. This introduces an undefined term into the contradicted section, leading to a contradiction as per the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: Operator shall comply in all respects with all Environmental Laws and all Environmental Permits.
perturbation.changed_text: Operator shall comply in all respects with all environmental regulations and all required authorizations.
perturbation.explanation: Section 4.1.1.5 originally mandates compliance with 'Environmental Laws' and 'Environmental Permits,' which are explicitly defined in Article I. By changing this to 'environmental regulations' and 'required authorizations,' the contract introduces undefined terms. This weakens the enforcement of environmental compliance since it's now ambiguous what specific laws and permits the Operator must adhere to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Operator shall perform its duties and obligations hereunder and its responsibilities as Operator of the Gathering System, (i) in a good and workmanlike manner, (ii) in conformity with the good practices in the natural gas gathering industry, (iii) in accordance with all valid and applicable Laws, including, without limitation, all Environmental Laws and Environmental Permits, (iv) in accordance with the Subject Gathering Agreements, and (v) in accordance with the Proposed Operating Expense Budget.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly references 'Environmental Laws' and 'Environmental Permits,' which are defined terms in Article I. The changed text replaces these with 'environmental regulations' and 'required authorizations,' which are undefined. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which specifically requires compliance with 'Environmental Laws and Environmental Permits.' By using undefined terms, the changed text creates ambiguity and weakens the obligation to comply with the defined laws and permits, leading to an in-text contradiction as the operator's duty to follow specific laws and permits is no longer clearly defined.

perturbation.original_text: Operator shall be responsible for preparing and submitting all regulatory filings pertaining to the Gathering System required by any Governmental Authority.
perturbation.changed_text: Service Provider shall be responsible for preparing and submitting all regulatory filings pertaining to the Gathering System required by any Public Agency.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Operator' to 'Service Provider' and 'Governmental Authority' to 'Public Agency' in Section 4.1.10, the contract introduces inconsistency. 'Operator' is defined in the preamble, and 'Governmental Authority' is defined in Article I. 'Service Provider' and 'Public Agency' are not defined, creating ambiguity about who is responsible for the filings and to which entities they must be submitted. This affects the clarity and enforceability of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Governmental Authority” means any federal, state, municipal, local or similar governmental authority, regulatory or administrative agency, court or arbitral body with jurisdiction over the Parties, this Agreement, any of the transactions contemplated hereby or the Gathering System.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Operator' with 'Service Provider' and 'Governmental Authority' with 'Public Agency'. While 'Operator' and 'Governmental Authority' are defined terms, 'Service Provider' and 'Public Agency' are not. The contradicted text explicitly defines 'Governmental Authority'. By introducing an undefined term 'Public Agency' to replace the defined term 'Governmental Authority', there is a clear contradiction, since the filing requirements and scope are now ambiguous. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology In-Text Contradiction where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
